data.narrative:
VEIR, a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $117M to date, operates within a competitive sector anchored by 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA). The peer set spans valuations from $120M to $5.0B, with a median of $355M across six same-stage, same-sector companies including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two valuation methods triangulate on VEIR's enterprise value. The stage-progression approach applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $117M raised proxy, yielding $222M. The peer-median method directly references the $355M median valuation across the six-company Climate & Energy Series B cohort. These methods produce implied values of $222M and $355M respectively.

Synthesizing across both approaches yields a valuation range of $189M (low) to $408M (high), with a median estimate of $289M. This range reflects meaningful dispersion within the Climate & Energy peer set—a sector characterized by high technical and commercialization risk. Key uncertainties include VEIR's technology differentiation relative to the broad peer cohort, capital efficiency versus peers, and path to revenue. The $5.0B ceiling in the comp set demonstrates significant upside optionality but also suggests wide valuation dispersion in this stage and sector.
